programmes.show_description: Comrades in Arms - Part 1: Lost behind enemy lines, Hawkeye and Margaret form a personal truce and seek shelter in a roadside hut.
Comrades in Arms - Part 2: Potter and BJ are frantic about their missing comrades leading BJ to disobey orders to find them. Hawkeye and Margaret have become more hostile towards each other.
The Merchant of Korea: BJ and Hawkeye challenge Major Winchester to a poker game in the hope that he will stop using the money they owe as leverage for random favours.
Paper Chase: Henry is concerned about Matthew's lack of effort towards his upcoming exams.
Rumpole and the Last Resort: Rumpole defends the owner of a caravan rental business who is accused of fraud. Meanwhile, Rumpole asks Hilda for help to get an elusive solicitor to pay a debt.
An uplifting round-up of good news stories from the UK and around the world.
The Good Doctor: While working with a professional football team, Jonathan and Mark learn that one of the players is hooked on drugs supplied by the team doctor.
An uplifting round-up of good news stories from the UK and around the world.
Alone: A homeless boy wishes that someone would love him.
An uplifting round-up of good news stories from the UK and around the world.

The Banker And Bum: Melvyn, a cold-hearted tycoon swaps places with Willie, a kind-hearted homeless man to see what it would be like if they each lived different lives.
The Brightest Star: Jonathan and Mark chasten Laurie, a young actress, whose fame is turning her into a monster, fracturing her family and robbing her of her childhood.
Injun Kid: Joseph Stokes is rejected by his grandpa because his daughter married a Native American. However, when Joseph faces some school bullies, his grandpa's opinion changes.
To Live with Fear: Part 1: Mary is seriously injured and in need of an expensive operation when she is accidentally kicked by a startled horse. Part 1 of 2.
To Live with Fear: Part 2: Charles accepts a dangerous job in order to pay for Mary's ongoing medical treatment. Part 2 of 2.
The Anniversary: John and Olivia celebrate their 25th wedding anniversary, and Mary Ellen and Curt both fight temptation during their enforced separation.
The Family Tree: With Jason's help, Verdie searches for her family history. Meanwhile, Elizabeth exaggerates the truth when writing to her soldier pen pal.
Love's Labours: When Frankie and Aidan join forces for a day out, Aidan's reputation as a priest is compromised. Liam and Donal are the first to hear a big announcement from Niamh and Sean.
The Element of Surprise: Jean invites Ruby to the Antiques Fair, where Ruby is shot in mysterious circumstances. As Deborah and Nick grow close, Henry offers Nick a job.
Robert's old military comrade Guy Le Saux is back in London, accompanied by his new young Canadian wife, Kathleen, to search out six desperate children for a fresh start in North America.
An uplifting round-up of good news stories from the UK and around the world.
Rumpole and the Old, Old, Story: Rumpole stays with the Erskine-Brown's after an argument with Hilda. Meanwhile, Rumpole defends a man accused of the attempted murder of his business partner.
The Smell of Music: Hawkeye and BJ refuse to shower until Major Winchester stops playing his French horn and Colonel Potter deals with a disfigured patient who wants to kill himself.
Patent 4077: Klinger accidentally discards Margaret's wedding ring and is sent to the dump to find it. Meanwhile, Hawkeye is fed up with having to work with inadequate surgical equipment.
Tea and Empathy: Penicillin has been stolen at the 4077th just as wounded soldiers arrive. Father Mulcahy knows where the penicillin is being stored due to a soldier's confession.
Old-school comedy chaos with Terry-Thomas, Joyce Grenfell and George Cole. A trip to Italy is up for grabs, and nothing is going to stop the terrors of St Trinian's raising hell in Europe... (1957)
An uplifting round-up of good news stories from the UK and around the world.
Old-school comedy chaos with Terry-Thomas, Joyce Grenfell and George Cole. A trip to Italy is up for grabs, and nothing is going to stop the terrors of St Trinian's raising hell in Europe... (1957)
The Seven Pound Fiddle: When money goes missing from the till, Nicholls blames Albert and sacks him. But Albert is not guilty, and it's hard to find him to apologise.
Ups and Downs: When they find a cellar under the bistro there are naturally disagreements between Robin and Nicholls as to what they should do with the space. More seating or a wine cellar?
The Smell of Music: Hawkeye and BJ refuse to shower until Major Winchester stops playing his French horn and Colonel Potter deals with a disfigured patient who wants to kill himself.
Patent 4077: Klinger accidentally discards Margaret's wedding ring and is sent to the dump to find it. Meanwhile, Hawkeye is fed up with having to work with inadequate surgical equipment.
Tea and Empathy: Penicillin has been stolen at the 4077th just as wounded soldiers arrive. Father Mulcahy knows where the penicillin is being stored due to a soldier's confession.
Bridge of Sighs: Henry's attention turns to the prospect of love and romance when his ex-girlfriend, who has been living in America for the last thirty years, returns to the UK for a visit.
An uplifting round-up of good news stories from the UK and around the world.
Rumpole and the Old, Old, Story: Rumpole stays with the Erskine-Brown's after an argument with Hilda. Meanwhile, Rumpole defends a man accused of the attempted murder of his business partner.
An uplifting round-up of good news stories from the UK and around the world.
Close Encounters of a Heavenly Kind: Jonathan and Mark offer encouragement to Adam, a bullied orphan whose eccentric grandfather is in danger of losing him to a heartless social worker.
An uplifting round-up of good news stories from the UK and around the world.
Change Of Life: Mark begins working for Linda Blackwell, a beautiful movie star with a very low opinion of men. They each 'wish to God' they could trade places, so God obliges them.
An uplifting round-up of good news stories from the UK and around the world.

...The Dear Children: Laura and Almanzo frantically search for their missing baby daughter, who they fear has been kidnapped. (1984)
The Wisdom of Solomon: Yearning to escape the hard life that sent his father to an early grave, eleven-year-old Solomon Henry runs away from his family and seeks solace with the Ingalls.
The Music Box: Nellie discovers that Laura has 'borrowed' a music box from her room and decides to blackmail her.
The Election: As a prank, the boys of Walnut Grove school nominate the object of their practical jokes, Elmer Dobkins, to run for class president against Mary Ingalls and Nellie Oleson.
The Ordeal: Part 1: Elizabeth falls while standing on top of a wood pile and fractures her leg. Part 1 of 2.
The Ordeal: Part 2: Elizabeth falls while standing on top of a wood pile and fractures her leg. Part 2 of 2.
The Wedding: Minto seizes his chance to give Dillon an unforgettable stag party, while a close encounter makes Niamh and Dillon's wedding day more memorable than expected.
A Wrathful God: When the local home for disadvantaged people is forced to close, Deborah takes in Eileen Fisher, one of the residents, but Eileen's admirer, Jimmy, follows suit.
Eleanor and Marsham rescue a carnival attraction, a young girl with 'the world's largest brain', who needs their help after being injured. Meanwhile, Eleanor's old nanny comes to visit.
An uplifting round-up of good news stories from the UK and around the world.
Rumpole and the Blind Tasting: Rumpole's latest case sees him defending Hugh Timson who is suspected of harbouring stolen wine. Meanwhile, Rumpole has concerns over his new pupil.
Your Hit Parade: A new shipment of records has come in and Col Potter has promised Radar that he can play the records over the PA system to lift everyone's spirits.
What's Up, Doc?: Margaret, believing she is pregnant, undergoes a rabbit test administered by Hawkeye. A patient who desperately wants to go home holds Charles and BJ at gunpoint.
Mail Call Three: Five sacks of mail arrive at the 4077th, three weeks late. Radar receives a letter from his mother informing him of her new boyfriend.
Dark thriller starring Denzel Washington and Angelina Jolie. Can a paralysed forensics expert and a rookie cop unravel a serial killer's cryptic crime-scene clues before he strikes again? (1999)
An uplifting round-up of good news stories from the UK and around the world.
Dark thriller starring Denzel Washington and Angelina Jolie. Can a paralysed forensics expert and a rookie cop unravel a serial killer's cryptic crime-scene clues before he strikes again? (1999)
Escape in Time: The Avengers attempt to track down criminals who have mysteriously vanished. Is the case linked to the deaths of secret agents and a Tudor mansion?
